_README: Cultural translation rules grouped by target-language code. Replace these baseline rules with the authoritative set from the cultural_translation_rules database table when available. The active cultural prompt looks up rules by the targetLang field on the segment (e.g. 'DE', 'de', 'German' all match the German bucket).

German.description: German B2B/B2C marketing is direct, fact-led, and restrained. Avoid 'best', 'world-class', 'unmatched', 'revolutionary' unless backed by specific cited data.

German.rule: Replace English idioms with natural German equivalents
German.description: Replace English metaphors, sports analogies, or American cultural references with natural German equivalents or plain rephrasing. Do not translate idioms literally.
Dates must be DD.MM.YYYY format (e.g. 24.10.2025). Thousands separator is '.', decimal separator is ',' (e.g. 1.500,75 not 1,500.75).
Convert any Imperial units to Metric: inches→cm, miles→km, lbs→kg, oz→g, °F→°C. Keep dosing units (mg, mL) as-is.
Use natural German call-to-action phrases like 'Jetzt anfragen', 'Mehr erfahren', 'Jetzt starten' instead of literal translations of 'Get started today' or 'Click here now'.
Maintain a formal register using the 'Sie' form (not 'du') aligned with German market expectations for professional B2B/B2C communication in pharma.
